batch_metadata.note: Phase A/B boundary batch. May-Jun 2025 continued escalation. Jun 7-8 harm allegation cluster (RF-04+RF-05 compound). Three-block data structure applies from B-024 forward.

arc_state_snapshot.note: ARC-RF07-001 ACTIVE. No new primary RF-07 signals this batch. Jun 7-8 harm allegation cluster (RF-04+RF-05 primary) assessed for RF-07 flooding component: cluster is significant in RF-04+RF-05 context but RF-07 flooding threshold confirmation requires timestamp granularity not available from RF-04 batch data for this specific episode. Outside the triage hot-period peak (May spike concentrated around May 4). Held without RF-07 primary flag.

batch_observations.tone_assessment: Elevated — May-Jun 2025 escalation continues. Jun 7-8 harm allegation cluster is a Phase B escalation marker. RF-04+RF-05 compound events present but RF-07 does not generate a primary signal this batch.
batch_observations.rf07_assessment: Zero RF-07 primary signals. Jun 7-8 cluster assessed but RF-07 flooding threshold not confirmable from available data. S-003 in B-024 represents the May 2025 hot-period anchor. This batch contains continued elevated communication but no confirmed flooding window or continuation-after-stop episode meeting the primary threshold.
batch_observations.custodial_context: May-Jun 2025 parenting-time weekends. Elevated contact volume but without a concentrated non-urgent cluster during identified parenting-time windows at ADD-004 threshold.
